disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began within hours of being outdoors, with intense bilateral ocular itch as the dominant feature
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when it started?
What does it feel like in your eyes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started after I was outside this afternoon. Both eyes are itching so intensely I can hardly stand it — the itch is the worst part.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Concurrent sneezing and clear watery rhinorrhoea began at the same time as the eye symptoms
trigger_question_examples: Any other symptoms — a runny nose or sneezing?
Is anything else bothering you apart from your eyes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I've been sneezing a lot and my nose is running clear and watery at the same time as my eyes started up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The discharge is strictly watery and clear, with no thick yellow/green purulence or lash matting
trigger_question_examples: Is the discharge watery and clear, or thick and yellow/green?
Are your eyelashes stuck together or crusted, especially in the morning?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's all clear and watery, just tears really. Nothing thick or yellow, and my lashes aren't stuck together.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No lip, tongue or throat swelling; no difficulty breathing, no widespread body hives, no faintness
trigger_question_examples: Is there any swelling of your lips, tongue or throat?
Any trouble breathing, tightness in the throat, a widespread rash, or feeling faint?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my lips, tongue and throat feel normal, I'm breathing fine, and I don't have a rash anywhere else or feel faint. It's really just my eyes and a runny nose.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't think so — should I be worried about that?

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No change in vision, no severe deep or aching eye pain, no light sensitivity
trigger_question_examples: Has your vision changed or gone blurry?
Do you have deep aching pain in the eye, or does bright light hurt?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My vision seems okay once I hold my eyes open — it's just watery. There's no deep aching pain, and bright light doesn't really hurt. It's the itching and swelling that's terrible.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Both eyes affected equally and symmetrically; no single painful protruding eye and no fever
trigger_question_examples: Is it both eyes equally, or is one eye worse?
Is one eye pushed forward or painful to move, and do you have a fever?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's both eyes exactly the same. Neither eye is pushed forward or painful to move, and I don't have a fever.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: History of seasonal atopy; this episode is unprecedented in severity for the patient
trigger_question_examples: Have you had anything like this before?
Do you get hay fever or allergies?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I get mild itchy eyes in spring sometimes, but never anything this bad or this fast.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rubbing the eyes worsens the symptoms; tap-water rinsing gave no relief
trigger_question_examples: Have you tried anything for it?
Does anything make it better or worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Rubbing them makes it flare up worse. I splashed tap water in them and it didn't help at all.
